claim: Chenodeosycholic acid treatment reduces whole-body energy expenditure.
evidence_sentences: In rodents, BAT can be activated by bile acids, which activate type 2 iodothyronine deiodinase (D2) in BAT via the G-coupled protein receptor TGR5, resulting in increased oxygen consumption and energy expenditure.
Treatment of 12 healthy female subjects with CDCA for 2 days resulted in increased BAT activity.
Whole-body energy expenditure was also increased upon CDCA treatment.
